title: chamaeiris marschalliana
intro: Chamaeiris marschalliana, also known as Marschall's clover or common bird's-foot trefoil, is a perennial legume in the pea family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a diuretic. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as aiding in the treatment of digestive issues and alleviating symptoms of menopause, due to its high content of isoflavones. Herbal preparations of Chamaeiris marschalliana include teas and infusions, often used to promote menstrual health and soothe digestive problems.
